title: × Dupoa labradorica
intro: Dupoa labradorica, also known as Labrador tea, is a plant species belonging to the heather family (Ericaceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dupoa labradorica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Labrador tea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory problems and as a natural remedy for cold and flu symptoms.

preparations: × Dupoa labradorica, also known as Labrador tea, has been used in herbal preparations for centuries, particularly by indigenous communities in North America. The leaves of the plant are typically harvested and made into a tea by infusing them in hot water. This tea is said to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In traditional medicine, Labrador tea has been used to treat a range of ailments, from sore throats and colds to digestive issues. The plant's leaves are also rich in vitamins and minerals, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.
